proposal: Pay civil servants in coupons instead of cash. Ledgers shrink. Merchants invent new complaints.

options.label: Issue store-credit coupons
options.result_text: Pay stubs become shop tickets. Clerks debate which aisle counts as wages.

options.label: Issue tradable coupons
options.result_text: Coupons circulate. A secondary wage market opens before lunch.

options.label: Keep paying in cash
options.result_text: Cash remains boringly legal. Penny redraws the austerity chart.

authoring.visual_hook: pay stubs printed as store coupons
authoring.writer_notes: Store-credit vs tradable branch flags for state-dependent market consequence.

proposal: Your coupon payroll changed how people spend. Store-credit staff barter groceries. Tradable coupons birthed a sidewalk exchange. Pick a fix before the ledger invents itself.

options.label: Claw back unused coupons
options.result_text: Expired coupons return to the vault. Morale files a late complaint.

options.label: Convert coupons to cash
options.result_text: Cash returns with interest in apologies. The sidewalk exchange closes.

options.label: Formalize the coupon economy
options.result_text: Coupons get serial numbers and a ministry desk. Profit smiles like a franchise.

authoring.visual_hook: sidewalk coupon exchange board
authoring.writer_notes: Consequence cites store-credit vs tradable implementation from setup.

proposal: Mask failing sewers with a controlled scent pilot. Cheaper than pipes. Public health remains a hypothesis.

options.label: Launch the scent pilot
options.result_text: Manholes exhale approved fragrance. Maybe schedules a follow-up 'maybe'.

options.label: Scent plus partial repairs
options.result_text: Some pipes improve. Scent covers the rest. Budgets look honest and tired.

options.label: Fund pipes, skip perfume
options.result_text: Pipes stop improvising. Maybe files the scent protocol under 'unused'.

authoring.visual_hook: manhole covers with scent diffuser stickers
authoring.writer_notes: Absorbs standalone perfumed_sewage_reform; fallout is health/budget not smell gag.

proposal: Your sewer scent program hid cracked pipes long enough for clinics to notice runoff. Neighbors want real infrastructure, not another fragrance invoice.

options.label: Fund real pipe repairs
options.result_text: Crews open streets. Clinics quiet down. Olga keeps the receipt.

options.label: Regulate scent dosage
options.result_text: Dosage charts appear on manholes. Runoff still exists, politely labeled.

options.label: Scrap the scent program
options.result_text: Diffusers unplug. The capital smells honest again. Maybe shrugs.

authoring.visual_hook: street crews beside labeled sewer vents
authoring.writer_notes: Public-health and budget consequence; references prior scent/repair choice.

proposal: Require Form F-0 to request any other form. Throughput becomes measurable. Citizens become stationery.

options.label: Enact Form F-0 nationwide
options.result_text: Form F-0 prints itself. Windows learn a new silence.

options.label: Pilot in one ministry
options.result_text: One ministry drowns politely. Other ministries take notes.

options.label: Keep forms un-nested
options.result_text: Forms remain merely numerous. Clerk Zero files disappointment as Form D-1.

authoring.visual_hook: desk stacked with Form F-0 pads
authoring.writer_notes: Hard backlog follow-up; efficiency resolution seeds government_by_form.

proposal: Your Form F-0 policy created a backlog of requests to request forms. Processing time is now measured in hallways. Attach Form F-0-A.

options.label: Hire form processors
options.result_text: New desks appear. The backlog relocates one floor down.

options.label: Add Form F-0-A
options.result_text: Nesting depth increases. Citizens bring camping chairs.

authoring.visual_hook: towers of nested form requests
authoring.writer_notes: Hard-forced escalation; soft to Penny resolution.

proposal: Form policy costs more than it sorts. Deepen the nest, buy real efficiency, or repeal F-0 before the hallway becomes a ministry.

options.label: Deepen the form nest
options.result_text: F-0 becomes culture. Clerk Zero files a quiet victory.

options.label: Buy real form efficiency
options.result_text: One form does three jobs. Elites invoice the miracle. Government by form begins.

options.label: Repeal Form F-0
options.result_text: Nested forms unstack. Hallways rediscover walking.

authoring.visual_hook: efficiency plaque beside a thinner form stack
authoring.writer_notes: Efficiency path seeds government_by_form ending; meaningful treasury/elite cost.

proposal: Back the currency with fish. Cats approve. Ledgers smell ambitious. This is not a market subsidy — it is monetary theory with scales.

options.label: Peg cash to fish stocks
options.result_text: Coins gain a faint marine confidence. Markets cheer, briefly.

options.label: Pilot fish scrip only
options.result_text: Scrip booths open near docks. Cats queue with professional dignity.

options.label: Keep boring metal money
options.result_text: Currency stays mineral. Whiskers drafts a dissenting meow.

authoring.visual_hook: dockside fish-scrip exchange booth
authoring.writer_notes: Blocked while fish_market_subsidy_act active; pool boom then resolution.

proposal: Your fish currency looked successful. Exchange boards glowed. Then the catch thinned and the peg wobbled. Unstable success needs a decision.

options.label: Ride the boom longer
options.result_text: Boards stay green for a week. Penny starts a contingency hymn.

options.label: Admit the peg is unstable
options.result_text: Honesty cools the booth. Cats respect the briefing.

authoring.visual_hook: wobbly fish exchange board
authoring.writer_notes: Initially successful unstable path via ride_the_fish_boom.

proposal: Stabilize the fish peg, raid the emergency reserve, or abandon fish money before wallets grow fins and panic.

options.label: Stabilize the fish peg
options.result_text: Peg holds with boring math. Whiskers accepts stability as a temporary revolution.

options.label: Tap fish emergency reserve
options.result_text: Reserve barrels open. Cats toast. The treasury looks thinner and prouder.

options.label: Abandon fish currency
options.result_text: Scrip booths close. A bank line forms with opinions and empty nets.

authoring.visual_hook: stabilizer chart next to empty fish crates
authoring.writer_notes: Abandon starts bank_run crisis; reserve path feeds cat_favor.

proposal: Elevate queues to a Ministry of Waiting. Waiting becomes policy. Service windows gain cabinet rank.

options.label: Found the ministry
options.result_text: A plaque appears above the longest line. Forms salute it.

options.label: Pilot one waiting office
options.result_text: One office perfects standing still. Other offices take notes.

options.label: Keep waiting unofficial
options.result_text: Queues remain folk tradition. Clerk Zero files Form W-0 unused.

authoring.visual_hook: cabinet plaque above a public queue
authoring.writer_notes: Soft service consequence affects Order/public services beyond Clerk affinity.

proposal: Your Ministry of Waiting rearranged service hours. Some desks open later so lines look intentional. Citizens want service, not a philosophy of standing.

options.label: Extend real service windows
options.result_text: Windows stay open. Lines shrink. Olga keeps a polite scoreboard.

options.label: Publish waiting etiquette
options.result_text: Posters teach heel angles. Order rises. Conversation falls.

options.label: Dissolve the ministry
options.result_text: The plaque comes down. Waiting returns to improvisation.

authoring.visual_hook: service window clocks and queue markers
authoring.writer_notes: Public-service/Order outcomes; distinct from robot queue kiosks.

proposal: Triple-stamp law meets an empty stamp drawer. Approvals freeze. Attach Form ST-1 if you can find ink.

options.label: Declare stamp emergency
options.result_text: Drawers open to air. Every desk becomes a bottleneck.

options.label: Ration remaining stamps
options.result_text: One stamp per crisis. Forms wait in alphabetical despair.

options.label: Suspend triple-stamp rule
options.result_text: One stamp returns to fashion. Clerk Zero underlines the temporary nature of relief.

authoring.visual_hook: empty official stamp drawer
authoring.writer_notes: Hard workaround; unconventional fix not meta-forms.

proposal: Your stamp shortage froze approvals. Profit proposes unconventional seals: wax futures, reusable seals, or thumbprint notaries. Pick before the drawer invents a black market.

options.label: Launch wax-seal futures
options.result_text: Wax trades open. Approvals resume with a melting point.

options.label: Authorize thumbprint seals
options.result_text: Ink pads return. Citizens leave unique marks. Forms look personal.

options.label: Import emergency stamps
options.result_text: Foreign stamps arrive with tourist slogans. Bureaucracy resumes at full postage.

authoring.visual_hook: wax seal futures board beside empty stamp tray
authoring.writer_notes: Unconventional administrative fixes; not Form F-0 nesting.

proposal: Hold a referendum on vacuum quiet hours. Cats call it dignity. Humans call it dust. Treat it as politics, not a punchline.

options.label: Schedule a full referendum
options.result_text: Ballots print with paw-optional boxes. Campaign season begins at low volume.

options.label: Pilot quiet blocks first
options.result_text: A few blocks go quiet at noon. Cats stretch like legislators.

options.label: Refuse the vacuum vote
options.result_text: Vacuums remain legal. Whiskers schedules a quieter protest.

authoring.visual_hook: quiet-hours posters with vacuum silhouettes
authoring.writer_notes: Pool campaign then resolution; cat_favor for cat_republic.

proposal: Your anti-vacuum referendum turned into a species dispute. Cat blocs claim dignity. Cleaning clubs claim dust rights. Public opinion wants a ruling that is not a punchline.

options.label: Amplify the cat bloc
options.result_text: Paw posters dominate. Vacuum shops hire lawyers.

options.label: Mediate the species debate
options.result_text: Town halls use soft voices. Dust remains bipartisan.

options.label: Spin cleaning as freedom
options.result_text: Broadcasts call noise 'civic polish.' Cats remain unconvinced.

authoring.visual_hook: referendum booth with cat and broom signs
authoring.writer_notes: State-dependent campaign flags for resolution callback.

proposal: Count the vacuum referendum. Full quiet-hours ban, timed compromise, or reject the cat platform before dust becomes dogma.

options.label: Enact vacuum quiet hours
options.result_text: Noon goes soft. Cats nap like legislators. Brooms wait their turn.

options.label: Compromise timed vacuums
options.result_text: Vacuums get office hours. Cats file a polite minority report.

options.label: Reject the vacuum platform
options.result_text: Vacuums roar again. A protest forms with both fur and dust masks.

authoring.visual_hook: quiet hours plaque beside a silent vacuum
authoring.writer_notes: Reject path starts mass_protest; cat options feed cat_republic.

proposal: Declare National Nap Hour. Happiness rises with pillows. Productivity negotiates with gravity. Not a science cot grid — a civic pause.

options.label: Mandate a full nap hour
options.result_text: Sirens soften into lullabies. Desks acquire official pillows.

options.label: Pilot official nap zones
options.result_text: Parks become quiet republics. Offices keep one eye open.

options.label: Refuse mandatory naps
options.result_text: Alarm clocks remain sovereign. Whiskers yawns in dissent.

authoring.visual_hook: civic nap hour banners over plazas
authoring.writer_notes: Blocked while nap_grid_live; soft productivity then compromise resolution.

proposal: Your National Nap Hour improved moods and delayed deliveries. Happiness purrs. Order yawns. Productivity wants a schedule that still ships forms.

options.label: Defend the social benefits
options.result_text: Citizens nap proudly. Couriers invent quieter routes.

options.label: Admit the productivity hit
options.result_text: Ledgers frown politely. Pillows remain under review.

authoring.visual_hook: empty desks beside stopped clocks
authoring.writer_notes: State-dependent flags for resolution; happiness vs Order trade.

proposal: Settle Nap Hour: keep the full mandate, pass a timed compromise, or cancel. An emergency rest option can hand morale to recovery protocols if the country is exhausted.

options.label: Keep the full nap mandate
options.result_text: The nation naps on schedule. History files it under soft power.

options.label: Pass timed nap compromise
options.result_text: Twenty quiet minutes, stamped and loved. Desks reopen on time.

options.label: Emergency civic rest link
options.result_text: Morale protocols wake. Smile Day recovery may appear later if happiness stays low enough to need it.

authoring.visual_hook: timed nap zones with compromise clocks
authoring.writer_notes: Compromise resolution required; emergency option soft-links recovery_national_smile_day.
